name: Taboada – tour around the Miño and Chantada rivers
name_original: Taboada – vuelta por río Miño y Chantada
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Taboada traverse a region characterized by the Miño River, its dramatic canyon, and the terraced vineyards of Ribeira Sacra. The area features an irregular orography, presenting both challenging ascents and rewarding descents. Routes often pass through historic towns and areas rich in Romanesque architecture, offering diverse landscapes for cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Taboada The most popular touring cycling route is Chantada - tour around the Miño River and Os Peares Dam Reservoir, a 43.6 miles (70.1 km) trail that takes 5 hours 9 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation changes along the Miño River. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Portomarín and Sarria Loop, a difficult 33.2 miles (53.5 km) path. This route connects historic towns with varied terrain and notable ascents. Local touring cyclists also love the Monte Faro Loop from Chantada, a 30.2 miles (48.5 km) trail leading through Monte Faro, often completed in about 3 hours 37 minutes. Touring cycling around Taboada is defined by the Miño River, Ribeira Sacra vineyards, and Romanesque architecture. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Taboada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.95 stars from over 16 reviews. More than 160 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Taboada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Taboada?
A: Taboada offers over 30 touring cycling routes, catering to various preferences. While most are considered difficult due to the region's irregular orography, there are also moderate options available for those seeking a less strenuous ride.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes in Taboada?
A: While the majority of routes around Taboada are challenging, there is at least one easy touring cycling route. The region's terrain, characterized by the Miño River and Ribeira Sacra, often involves significant ascents and descents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Taboada?
A: Touring cycling routes in Taboada feature an irregular orography with both challenging ascents and rewarding descents. You'll encounter a mix of asphalted roads and natural paths, often following the Miño River and passing through the terraced vineyards of Ribeira Sacra. Some sections, especially those connected to the Camino de Santiago, may include medieval roads.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Taboada?
A: Yes, Taboada offers several circular touring cycling routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017432730/monte-faro-loop-from-chantada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Faro Loop from Chantada&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, leading through the scenic Monte Faro. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017348492/portomarin-and-sarria-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portomarín and Sarria Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which connects historic towns.

Q: What are the best times of year for touring cycling in Taboada?
A: The region around Taboada, with its diverse landscapes and Romanesque heritage, is generally pleasant for cycling during spring and autumn. These seasons offer milder temperatures, which are ideal for tackling the area's significant elevation changes and enjoying the scenic views along the Miño River and Ribeira Sacra.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Along the touring cycling routes, you can experience the stunning landscapes of the Miño Canyon and the terraced vineyards of the Ribeira Sacra region. Notable natural highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6496544" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira Sacra River Valley&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5795615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miño River&lt;/a&gt; itself, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5613861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ribeira Sacra and Serras do Oribio and Courel Biosphere Reserve&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5613862" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Misty Forest With Ivy-Covered Willows&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to explore while cycling?
A: Yes, Taboada is rich in historical and cultural heritage. Many routes pass through areas with Romanesque architecture, including ancient monasteries and churches. You can also encounter landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/751631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portomarín Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/137061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of St John of Portomarín&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/770706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castromaior Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;. Sections of the Camino de Santiago, with markers like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/769964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;100 km Marker&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4497257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Shell-Shaped Fountain&lt;/a&gt;, also offer cultural insights.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Taboada?
A: The touring cycling routes in Taboada are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average score of 4.95 stars from over 16 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the challenging yet rewarding terrain, and the opportunity to explore areas rich in Romanesque architecture and natural beauty along the Miño River.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the Miño River?
A: Absolutely. The Miño River is a defining feature of the region, and many touring cycling routes incorporate its scenic banks. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017341434/chantada-tour-around-the-mino-river-and-os-peares-dam-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chantada - tour around the Miño River and Os Peares Dam Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which offers significant elevation changes alongside the river. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018478404/taboada-tour-around-the-mino-and-chantada-rivers" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Taboada – tour around the Miño and Chantada rivers&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Taboada suitable for family-friendly touring cycling?
A: While many routes in Taboada are rated as difficult due to significant elevation changes, the region does offer some options that might be more suitable for families or leisure cyclists. It's recommended to check individual route details for difficulty and terrain to ensure it aligns with your family's experience level. The natural beauty and cultural sites can be enjoyed by all.

Q: Are there specific routes that are part of the Camino de Santiago?
A: Yes, parts of the Camino de Santiago pass through the Taboada area, offering cyclists a chance to experience this historic pilgrimage route. While some sections of the Camino can be technical with medieval roads, they are generally suitable for gravel, hybrid, and mountain bikes, and can be incorporated into touring cycling plans.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Taboada?
A: Specific parking information for each route start point is often available within the komoot tour details. Generally, towns like Chantada, which serve as common starting points for many routes, offer parking facilities. It's advisable to check the individual route pages for precise parking recommendations.
tips:
Near Taboada is the church of San Pedro de Bembibre,
The first writing about it is preserved from the 10th century. A good place to rest on a warm day.
Steel bridge over the Belesar reservoir over the Miño river in the Ribera Sacra natural park. When the water levels are low you can see the old submerged bridge.
Stone watchtower from the 12th century located in the Ribeira Sacra. It is considered one of the oldest buildings in the region.
Chantada is a regional commercial city located on the left bank of the Asma River, halfway along the commercial route between Ourense and Lugo.

waypoints.name: Church of San Pedro de Bembibre
